scenario: Hard lockup due to timer discrepancy and spinlock deadlock
symptoms: Watchdog detected hard LOCKUP on cpu 22
Kernel panic - not syncing: Hard LOCKUP
Task looping in spinlock in kernel mode for >60 seconds
tvec_base values way behind current jiffies on all CPUs
Multiple tasks stuck in I/O wait on lv_disk04

workflow.thought: Check system information and BIOS version
workflow.observation: BIOS date and version for hardware compatibility

workflow.thought: Examine tasks in uninterruptible sleep state
workflow.observation: Tasks stuck in I/O wait on CPU 22

workflow.action: foreach UN bt | awk '/#[1-4] /{print $3,$5}' | paste - - - - | column -t

workflow.observation: Active SCSI commands with negative deadlines

workflow.thought: Check request queue timeout timer status
Examine timer base jiffies and next timer
workflow.observation: timer_jiffies value compared to current jiffies

workflow.thought: Check which CPU owns specific tvec_base

workflow.observation: CPU idle state and stack trace
Spinlock raw_lock value and waiter count

workflow.thought: Disassemble around specific address with source lines
Generate timer jiffies comparison for all CPUs
workflow.observation: Create command file for timer checks

workflow.action: < tjiffies | paste - -  | awk '{print NR "\t" $0 "\n"}' | awk '{if($NF<4479229141){print}}' | awk '$0!=""' | awk '{print $0 "\t" 4479229141-$NF " behind"}'
workflow.observation: Timer jiffies lag behind current jiffies

workflow.thought: Count exception RIP occurrences across all CPUs
workflow.action: bt -a | grep "exception RIP:" | sort | uniq -c | sort -k1nr

workflow.thought: Check watchdog thread CPU affinity masks
workflow.action: foreach 'watchdog/*' task -R cpus_allowed.bits | paste - - - | awk '$8~/watchdog\//' | awk '{c="";for(i=1;i<12;i++)c=c $i" ";print c}'

root_cause_analysis: The exact root cause is unknown at this time.
The hard lockup occurred on the CPU on which the task is looping in the spinlock in kernel mode without giving other tasks a chance to run for more than 60 seconds.
At that time another task had taken that spinlock and was running CPU idle routine.
At that time, almost all CPUs' tvec_base values were way behind the current jiffies value with the only exception of the one CPU on which the hard lockup occurred.
